sanskrit: यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते। सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते।।
transliteration: yadā hi nendriyārtheṣu na karmasv anuṣajjate | sarva-saṅkalpa-sannyāsī yogārūḍhas tadocyate ||
literal_meaning: When one no longer clings to sense-objects or fruitive actions, having renounced all material resolves, then he is said to be ‘yoked’ (yogārūḍha).
contextual_meaning: True inner stability arises when the mind’s automatic pull toward pleasure-seeking and outcome-chasing is seen through and released; this is the psychological tipping-point where practice becomes embodied.
psychological_state: Post-decisional dissonance reduction—mind shifts from compulsive doing to being-centered intentionality.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media for the tenth time, noticing the hollow after-taste, and choosing to set the phone down without needing to announce the detox.
anchor_text: Let go of craving and control; steady mind finds freedom.
